name: Monegros, the Spanish Western — circular gravel from the Monastery of Sigena
name_original: Monegros, el Western español — gravel circular desde el Monasterio de Sigena
region_intro: Touring cycling around Villanueva de Sigena offers routes through the distinctive arid terrain of the Monegros region, characterized by ochre hues and unique rock formations known as "tozales." The landscape includes the semi-desert environment, with the Sierra de Alcubierre providing varied topography and hills. The Alcanadre river valley forms a small oasis, contrasting with the surrounding dry plains. This area provides a solitary and expansive cycling experience. Best touring cycling routes around Villanueva De Sigena The most popular touring cycling route is Vuelta al Monegros — circular desde Sariñena, a 51.8 miles (83.3 km) trail that takes 7 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses the expansive Monegros desert landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Monegros, the Spanish Western — circular gravel from the Monastery of Sigena, a moderate 24.8 miles (39.9 km) path. This route features gravel sections through the Monegros, starting from the historic Monastery of Sigena. Local touring cyclists also love the Laguna de Sariñena — circular from Sariñena, a 15.8 miles (25.4 km) trail leading through the Laguna de Sariñena wildlife refuge, often completed in about 1 hour 37 minutes. Touring cycling around Villanueva de Sigena is defined by the arid Monegros desert, unique "tozales" rock formations, and the contrasting green of the Alcanadre river valley. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Villanueva de Sigena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from 6 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Villanueva de Sigena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Villanueva de Sigena?
A: The touring cycling routes around Villanueva de Sigena primarily traverse the distinctive arid terrain of the Monegros region. You'll encounter ochre hues, unique rock formations known as "tozales," and a semi-desert environment. The Sierra de Alcubierre offers varied topography and hills, while the Alcanadre river valley provides a contrasting green oasis. This landscape offers a solitary and expansive cycling experience.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are easy touring cycling routes available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980280092/laguna-de-sarinena-circular-from-sarinena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Sariñena — circular from Sariñena&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 15.8-mile (25.4 km) trail that takes you through the Laguna de Sariñena wildlife refuge, offering a gentler ride.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see along the cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in unique natural features. You can encounter fascinating rock formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5771760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de la Cobeta&lt;/a&gt; and the broader &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5228497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozales de Monegros&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2105057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pilón (Sierra de Sena Summit)&lt;/a&gt; offers rewarding views after a climb. The Alcanadre river valley also provides a green contrast to the arid landscape.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as circular tours, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971333335/monegros-the-spanish-western-circular-gravel-from-the-monastery-of-sigena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monegros, the Spanish Western — circular gravel from the Monastery of Sigena&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 24.8-mile (39.9 km) path featuring gravel sections.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Villanueva de Sigena?
A: The Monegros region experiences hot summers and can be quite windy. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. This allows for a more comfortable exploration of the arid landscapes.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites I can visit while cycling?
A: Absolutely. The Royal Monastery of Santa María de Sigena, an ancient and significant religious building, is located on the outskirts of town and can be easily incorporated into your ride. Additionally, the Michael Servetus Interpretation Center, the birthplace of the physician and theologian, offers a cultural stop for those interested in history.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Villanueva de Sigena?
A: The touring cycling routes in Villanueva de Sigena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars. Cyclists often praise the unique arid landscape, the sense of solitude, and the variety of routes that cater to different skill levels, from easy to difficult. The opportunity to explore both natural wonders and historical sites is also a highlight.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379789762/vuelta-al-monegros-circular-desde-sarinena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta al Monegros — circular desde Sariñena&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 51.8-mile (83.3 km) trail that traverses the expansive Monegros desert landscape, offering a demanding yet rewarding experience.

Q: Where can I find parking for my car when starting a cycling tour?
A: While specific parking locations for each route vary, Villanueva de Sigena and nearby towns like Sariñena typically offer public parking areas suitable for leaving your vehicle while you embark on your cycling tour. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for designated parking zones.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Royal Monastery of Santa María de Sigena?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24551838" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sijena Monastery – Sigena Monastery / Sixena loop from Sena&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 33.9-mile (54.6 km) route that specifically includes the Monastery of Sigena, allowing you to combine your ride with a visit to this historic site.

Q: Can I find viewpoints along the touring cycling routes to enjoy the scenery?
A: Yes, the region offers several viewpoints that enhance the cycling experience. Mirador de la Mora, Mirador del Pilón (offering views of the entire Huesca province), and Mirador de la Sierra provide excellent vistas of the valley stretching to the Pyrenees. Mirador del Tozal de Macarier, located in a green corridor, showcases the extensive forest mass.

Q: What is the total number of touring cycling routes available in the Villanueva de Sigena area?
A: There are 15 touring cycling routes documented around Villanueva de Sigena, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.
tips: The Sijena monastery and its church are a Cistercian monastery from the 11th century and are part of the town of Villanueva de Sigena. In recent years this monastery has not been exempt from controversy due to its highly valued paintings and historical figures.

The Monegros tozales are curious multi-colored rock formations, which, sculpted by erosion over the centuries, make up the soul of the essential spot in the Monegros desert. From this point and a few meters away, there are a few of them: Tozal Colasico, Tozal Solitario and Tozales de los Pedregales among others.
Uphill to the top of the Sierra de Presiñena, an area of mountains with little vegetation and where some forms appear on the slopes typical of the lands of the "Monegros".
